name: La Marina Baja along the coast — Tour from Altea
name_original: La Marina Baja por la costa — Tour desde Altea
region_intro: Road cycling around Altea offers a diverse landscape, combining coastal roads with challenging mountainous terrain. The region features rolling hills, steep mountain passes like Sierra de Bernia and Coll de Rates, and valleys with citrus orchards. This varied topography provides options for different skill levels, from flat coastal paths to significant climbs. Best road cycling routes around Altea The most popular road cycling route is La Marina Baja along the coast — Tour from Altea, a 31.0 miles (49.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) – Sa Creueta Pass loop from Garganes, a difficult 69.6 miles (112.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through a mountain pass, providing panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the View of Altea and Marina – Moraira Beach loop from Olla Altea, a 27.5 miles (44.2 km) trail leading through coastal areas with views of Altea and Moraira, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Altea is defined by coastal roads, rolling hills, and mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Altea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 8,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Altea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Altea?
A: Altea offers a highly diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll find a mix of coastal roads with stunning sea views, rolling hills, and challenging mountain passes like Sierra de Bernia and Coll de Rates. The region also features valleys with citrus orchards and almond groves, providing varied scenery for all types of riders.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Altea on komoot?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Altea documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to very difficult mountain challenges.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Altea?
A: Yes, Altea is known for its challenging mountain passes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32902837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) – Sa Creueta Pass loop from Garganes&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 112 km route with nearly 2000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38238504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Vall de Pop – Coll de Rates loop from Garganes&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the iconic Coll de Rates climb.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in Altea?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking less strenuous options, Altea offers easy coastal rides with stunning sea views. Routes like the Altea to Calpe or Altea to Albir coastal paths are mostly flat and well-paved, perfect for beginners, families, or a relaxed ride. The promenade along the coast is also ideal for leisurely cycling.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Altea?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views. The Sierra de Bernia provides unparalleled panoramic views of the coastline, and the Coll de Rates summit offers vistas that can stretch all the way to Ibiza on clear days. You can also find stunning Mediterranean views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/889730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Salinas de Calpe&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7344015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Calp Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33418738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polop Viewpoint – Polop de la Marina loop from Olla Altea&lt;/a&gt; also offers great scenery.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Altea?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Altea are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32902837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Creueta Pass (780 m) – Sa Creueta Pass loop from Garganes&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Altea and Marina – Moraira Beach loop from Olla Altea&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Altea?
A: Altea enjoys a perfect Mediterranean climate year-round, making it suitable for road cycling even outside the summer months. This mild climate contributes to its popularity as a training ground for professional cyclists, meaning you can enjoy great conditions almost any time.

Q: Are there any natural attractions like waterfalls along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the Algar River Valley offers routes that wind inland through citrus orchards. The Fonts de l'Algar waterfalls, just 3 km from Callosa d'en Sarrià, provide a refreshing stop and are a notable natural attraction in the area.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Altea?
A: The road cycling routes in Altea are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from stunning coastal views to challenging mountain climbs, and the well-maintained asphalt roads.

Q: Are there places to explore beyond cycling, like cultural landmarks?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, Altea's charming old town with its whitewashed houses, cobbled streets, and the iconic blue-domed Church of Nuestra Señora del Consuelo, provides a beautiful backdrop and cultural experience. Viewpoints within the old town offer stunning panoramas of the Mediterranean.

Q: Are there any specific highlights or landmarks to look out for on the routes?
A: Yes, besides the natural beauty, you might encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/174441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Ifach summit&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/892970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Mount d'Olta (587 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra de Bernia also features the historic Bernia Fort.
tips: The nicer part of the beach starts a little further back. Here you're near the harbor. You can drive on the promenade, but it's not really allowed. It's doable, though!
